textByHash.96ada8b2403188c8c12166e990c38c019e2993a4: Howdy stranger. What brings you out to these parts?
textByHash.455d91c7dd3b8e601e8c340a551ed60a6a52d5c7: Well, that's where you'd be wrong. I work for the Archaeological Society of Varrock, and have been excavating around here recently.
textByHash.0ba4de03ec93a18675bb72334dd725fdd35807d0: Was there something you specifically wanted?
textByHash.960aee576f20da4679839945ee5342643749cfce: Well, it's funny you should say that. I'm not sure if I would really call it a quest as such, but I found this ancient stone tablet in one of my excavations, and it would really help me out if you could go and take it back to the digsite for me and get it examined.
textByHash.47220b53ad2065ebe966d97dee8ebcb0c6e0f8c9: It's very old, and I don't recognise any of the inscriptions on it.
textByHash.e1ed080cb203572c212d94b8803ed17e466c3582: His name's Terry Balando. Give it to nobody but him. I'm sorry, I can't entrust you with the actual tablet I found, it is far too valuable to give away, but I took these etchings - they should be enough for him to make
textByHash.a7b9b216857ff5e17b224e2b1977f47eeffd3db8: a preliminary translation on. Come back and let me know what he says, I would hate to waste my time excavating anything that isn't worth my time as a world famous archaeologist!
textByHash.36ea2f0fb021b760d0115a6d353da4fbffb9dbb8: Well, okay. Can't say as I blame you for thinking that. I'll go take it there myself later I guess.
textByHash.f1baccc7007613c7d95069d4310c6a0380faecd0: You don't recognise me??? I am the world famous Asgarnia Smith, Archaeologist extraordinaire!
textByHash.615bfe78154291efde25294a897baf09456ea076: I am the one who discovered the long forgotten Temple of Ikov! The one who unearthed the strange trap filled arena in Brimhaven!
textByHash.50fb95cefc6016b1475cb0c5bbfa17f05f302667: I'm the leading archaeological expert of our time! I was voted archaeologist of the year four years running by the Varrock Herald! Are you REALLY sure you've never heard of me?
textByHash.c953048229a554fbd8009e3a29ca725bba9cb560: Well then, I'm confused. Why did you come over and speak to me if you don't know who I am? What do you want?
textByHash.39c346471332629aeddb39af4a8a919aaf3fc703: Uh-huh. Well, if you'll excuse me, I have a lot more work to do before I can bed down for the night.
textByHash.b5dd0a0e219c6c3b9991f786d5b000b32658a78f: So what did Terry Balando say about those etchings? Did he give you a translation for me?
textByHash.d598af97ea4147f52180b4cfa355ec6e42cf4013: Well what are you waiting for? A written invitation? All I want you to do is take those etchings up to the digsite for me!
textByHash.68b2873405e70fda2753cff8e080e050597d5d6e: Well how in the world did you do that? (sigh) Okay, I'll make another set, but try not to lose these ones this time!
textByHash.aa2e310e38fb6fac00b4eb8dda9e211c7c653360: Well could you go and get me that translation? It could be vital!
textByHash.b418f0a32952572bb3eb97d1d2dece78f2c7c00d: Did you take a ready of this? It will do you good to understand how this profession works. Here, have a read.
textByHash.24a31005d74ebcc15c9b5984c4614e06c83cc903: Excellent. Just give me a moment to read this, and talk to me again in a second.
textByHash.4fa67995a26da706ecd4fced30e6560747f6307e: Hmmm. Interesting. It seems to me like there's some kind of treasure hidden out in the desert.
textByHash.acff8f71306c7b3196044614f1e4e14aef2bcb3d: So what do you say? Fancy being a treasure hunter like me?
textByHash.a0fb5fa7a554735dac5e3a620e0cec8b443886ca: It's all this hot sun getting to me I think.
textByHash.6d1a2840a5cefaf3bbdbf4ef40ea0d323f08aa8c: Well anyway, let's just assume there is a treasure hidden in the desert somewhere, and let's just say for the sake of argument that maybe if I found a big stash of gold and treasure I wouldn't necessarily just hand it
textByHash.91e8e22d9dfa804b0a6b4004dd766429c990da12: all over to the Museum of Varrock.
textByHash.17caaa07aab885d12f9edcf0ef4423e3fe0d62b5: Let's also say, purely hypothetically, that if there were such a big stash of treasure and someone were to help me find it, then that hypothetical personal might be entitled to, oh, let's say a purely for the sake of
textByHash.a569ebc03ff4baebc24ce99a70b9543b22d4478d: A purely for the sake of argument fifty-fifty split on this hypothetical treasure, should it exist...
textByHash.15a9d6201d03113216f557c8e1d67f2c0cacc656: Do you see where I'm going with this?
textByHash.1a25c03ca683fa010c68e30143c83f0cdf33f8e9: Uh... yes, but the way you said it makes it sound like I'm doing something wrong...
textByHash.37e4ecf94d26b7cfcc18631aa6cd817f4beda24b: So what do you say? Partners?
textByHash.9f8fc01175ca92f0deabf0dc75351a2c4ce9bcde: Good lad! Well, if we split up we'll be able to find treasure quicker.
textByHash.a1ccece8a77f610ebede950aca1eb3e4f3001e1d: I'll continue searching around here in this Bedabin Camp, you head due South to the Bandit Village. If either of us find anything, we'll come find each other and say what, okay?
textByHash.54c2fcf8acac898fae7b75872e09a2eb9262ea6c: You head due South, see what you can find out about this tablet.
textByHash.90505b9ae3c94eaf3d4bcf8cfb8099ba87bcfd9a: Aw, c'mon, cut me a break here man! Look, nobody is really getting hurt, right? This treasure is either going to lie around in some underground ruin, or lie around in some museum for
textByHash.2365375fa603e0b93a71a8000d2bcb22e9684ab0: It makes sense for the people to go to all the risk to get a little payback for it, right? Besides, anything of real historical value we can hand over to the museum - we'll just keep the cash for
textByHash.e6a5ee10f9d122399977d1d57b7f6d013311fb33: C'mon... what do you say? Partners?
textByHash.8bbac92438eda20207f372605d1fdc322a474d03: Eh, you'll come running back when you realize just how much money we could be talking here.
textByHash.9df92f8c3f3af1661765f7ab9a2b0e764e35b034: I'll see you again when you come crawling back, begging to be cut in on the deal.
textByHash.8122c08054d1c5c9c56cc4fbaeb0276504bf8c29: Hello again. You find any signs of that treasure yet?
textByHash.a73211407ab4d0cfa77adf87d6b786c4bba871af: Well, head south to that bandit camp. I bet if there's anything out here, they'll know about it.
textByHash.ef264a48d145b9af4155e173c2a8234cf82c961e: Don't let on it might be valuable though! Those thieves will steal it as soon as look at you!
textByHash.545f1174b6e87ed42f66f1b16aa7dcf98dd49d32: Diamonds of Azzanadra? That sounds very promising indeed!
textByHash.e407ddfc36d447f880c9d722256ad29f75c17446: Nope, never heard of them before, but you said diamonds! Diamonds are always promising!
textByHash.f7b46d5c1714fe217816ced4b3d9e636156a3fbb: Hello again. How's the treasure hunting going?
textByHash.bb648d29ede8ed06f007fc0f1924815aecc70cfe: What? But you've been gone ages!
textByHash.aeac0d5f416e1c2ca6c480b7a7278d2767713afc: C'mon, you can be square with me, you must have found more treasure than just four measly diamonds by now!
textByHash.e3fd05a5057f48cf876ff721ebc0adddd6d5d800: Hmmm. Well, okay a deal is a deal. Hand two of them over.
textByHash.19fcd4dd759f7656cface5e74b0f989923edfd6f: Don't worry about two measly diamonds if you think they'll lead to something better!
textByHash.1aae1db084165b14ebded0e61c9c82e741847b30: I don't usually get out of bed for less than a hundred gold certs!
textByHash.0c46d080e5e9b96f9ac108f524cb69348d032b52: Hello again, apprentice archaeologist. How's the treasure hunting going?
textByHash.c786aab075a7e35acbb350850f96b7a558b6fce8: Great! Pass me my share, and it's a good job all around!
textByHash.0d7b1e3b05bc2374973331d8346bea7283070b52: Huh? You're not trying to break a deal here are ya???
textByHash.a75d99a0717a30957dcd49e5d3261428a49a7170: That's a crazy story kid. Why would you go putting diamonds into them in the first place anyway? Diamonds!
textByHash.36ddae9ff1cf3f6995c2ab0f0507f6a6c01d5b3b: Well what are you wasting time talking to me for?
textByHash.860ee32a31e4d755e4751a49a91bba9c9a23c8da: Go get me some treasure, pal!
textByHash.3e570b889d9300a495c2c972b1d20efb5a7db81e: You don't remember my name?! You don't remember the world famous Asgarnia Smith, archaeologist extraordinaire?
textByHash.2c16c87eaa463212bb88d80f93ce8aed2ac71ffb: All that work we did together, and you don't even remember my name.
textByHash.459069da4d3842f31250489c00c628911d6d93bd: And a good job I didn't! I never saw a single ounce of gold from that partnership.
textByHash.12107c21cf13fcff93195aa35cb9bc84b80ab3b9: Bah, I'm not here for you, kid. I'm here for treasure!
textByHash.025539d12fd7720395186237494e989905fa9cc9: After our last escapade turned out to be pointless, I set out in search of a new adventure! I started off on Karamja, but that didn't really go anywhere...
textByHash.4f6b02009cace7a9d03b6ae6fe2b799cded0599f: In the end, I came back to the desert and dug up this place! I've spent ages trying to work out how to get in, only for you to walk up and open the door right away!
textByHash.3aab869660f1975099fac8e975391a5f3792e129: Alright then, kid, I'll tell you what... You didn't find us any proper treasure last time around, so now's your chance to redeem yourself!
textByHash.df4aa7b5650ff0dec0a3a92e84f4a53d77698f70: I'm sure this place is the key to finding riches beyond imagination, and I, Asgarnia Smith, am willing to enter into a partnership with you, er...
textByHash.0b41bc7341aabf1a67f482306cf76df8e9998c72: Yes, as I was saying, I, Asgarnia Smith, am willing to enter into a partnership with you, , to uncover those riches!
textByHash.8914e13462111c4cfaa756bef28220e75d253de4: Who knows, perhaps you'll even learn a thing or two from a master treasure hun- I mean, a master archaeologist like me.
textByHash.8f149b9ada0ab3d1272da4474302ec5826133e00: And I am willing to offer you an exclusive rate of a forty percent split!
textByHash.e73b6975d12ec1e63e33fd73558ad22468254add: Absolutely! A forty-five percent split! So, kid, what do you say?
textByHash.71edc0dd5b43466388b384f6b5a3a8ae3dce86de: Well, kid, any thoughts on my offer?
textByHash.ae32ec31d0835500f3ff7141e273bd98efec04b2: Of course! Take as much time as you need, as long as you don't take long.
textByHash.69e7e14f1eb0d91d0077d4030789fe74d6d623ed: Mighty treasures, you say? Fantastic! Any idea how to access them?
textByHash.ae0a73848845647542485913bd3463dd71b9ffbd: Ah, so we need to find whatever it is they should be holding. Classic treasure vault puzzle! Where do you plan to start?
textByHash.c88b6ebf81c5998fe4a4674bce8d03b5e9652af1: But surely you can't turn down an opportunity like this! You said it yourself: Mighty treasures!
textByHash.873808e1194130503159578b3466181f2a538984: Now look here, kid. You won't get far in life with an attitude like that. Is there really nothing else we have to go on?
textByHash.7c07c89eacf929e569fbc302e9019f01a71fcfe0: They sound like pretty solid leads to me. If we can find these four individuals, maybe we can also find whatever it is we need to get to the treasure.
textByHash.5d2ad221f51b277390e7a8b9515c041bebd4d5f1: Zarosian? I'm sure I've heard that term before...
textByHash.ab83550cbb5a4ae46112d7a44d44883b9fe66677: Ah yes! Those recent discoveries at the Digsite! I bet my good friend Terry Balando can help. Why don't you talk to him?
textByHash.5f46bf86ac03567c04366f35d79c0c828ca4f69d: No, but you're going to. I can tell. The thrill of the adventure is too much to resist. Not to mention that thirty percent split!
textByHash.7457bd824425843ee1023b9dc04319d91fdc986a: So we're agreed? Excellent! You'll find Terry at the Digsite Exam Centre. Good luck!
textByHash.d06ca97087fca9404f485a9b209f98570f6c00cf: Good to see you, kid. Did you speak to Terry Balando?
textByHash.8d2b078bb93cac88217d4f6ed642c570d8585ea7: You'll find Terry at the Digsite Exam Centre. You'd best get going. There's treasure to uncover!
textByHash.e294801d7035f179ffcd2d6b2458cff531b400ac: Good to see you, kid. Have you had any luck with these statues yet?
textByHash.fe82cf508b664bbc2f9fb5528f5e5002418c8336: Well what are you waiting for? Go and give it to the statue! That twenty percent split won't earn itself.
textByHash.8b4d8d09fa358c87383ce489c5ef912767622249: Nonsense! Only a fool would say no to such a lucrative partnership, and you're clearly no fool.
textByHash.04e645b469ed1e11ac82be31f525cea8b0c419c4: Good to see you, kid. Nice work on that first statue. That treasure will be ours in no time.
textByHash.36ca5b5c81604c4766b7dc3505dc3464410d39b8: Good to see you, kid. Nice work on those two statues. That treasure will be ours in no time.
textByHash.7e6d6ffdf7c36ffb223c68f951434aaa1dbbfc1a: We're getting close now, kid. Just one more statue to go! I can smell that treasure already!
textByHash.bbd7512e9898d8d6071f19cea8a8923f5334172e: Nonsense! You'd be a fool to turn away now that we're so close! I'm about to make history - and you'll have helped!
textByHash.71dba5968d91ef979a004277be1b326d3bb7de4a: Blimey, kid. You don't half take your time, do you? That treasure of ours won't uncover itself!
textByHash.6841c0a0155a1e5da947a8eedc5f9ee71e7f8b69: What? You're not thinking of double-crossing me, are you?
textByHash.50fe879379a21d43c725104015123dc47c9881ad: Oh, I see what's happening! You're trying to trick me so you can take the treasure for yourself!
textByHash.f4553c86915727077a9a07f4593de7d1e39c7ee8: Well of course there will be others who want the treasure! All the more reason for you to stop messing around.
textByHash.cc08b92ed241da6337d41a06af02b3984c97fd41: I really thought you had what it takes, kid, but you're just another fool. If you won't help me find the treasure, I'll just have to do it myself!
textByHash.ecd911ef2c02ed5423c139bfa77f5593832d2f2b: Do you feel that? There's a power radiating off it unlike anything I've felt before...
textByHash.2827309a246f0a7e7f2551a6a1e852f7b4dfde4d: So you know what it is?
textByHash.4044f756a0b0c1e071fad4a09397f1fb2d3319f0: Well, it's funny you should say that. I'm not sure if I would really call it a quest as such, but I found this ancient stone tablet in one of my excavations, and it would really help me out if you
textByHash.08b006fc8a32f20c7e4573ecf9873854677b6c8c: could go and take it back to the digsite for me and get it examined.
textByHash.9a495e35b89e425cad91f096520554a7ea93ab9f: Aw... I was hoping to go abroad on holiday this year.
textByHash.5db2d5ecb969bb3413beb091f1f7c9a71ee0d483: Maybe I'll go check out Karamja next, I hear there's treasure to be found there...
textByHash.879478763df7d9752a842e0b09f3a0a03956858b: Hey, sure thing kid! Well, this one time I found this spooky old temple in the middle of the jungle.
textByHash.59ad683701dcb8b5b170f88780e8eabfc9ebeda0: The entrance was hidden from sight by all these vines and stuff, and it was full of zombies and crazy tribesmen and stuff.
textByHash.c48c384acf2481a7be633fa41ade13a7f9ce08fc: That wasn't the worst thing though! There were all these crazy traps that were hidden around the place too!
textByHash.1559a92a9d4040ab002cdb7b7bf9fac08a9b5158: Well anyway, I found the secret entrance, fought my way in through the zombies and tribesmen, and came to a big pedestal with a statue of solid gold sat on top of it!
textByHash.4a8b488017706ec7e63bfb7083d83a72dc78e8fe: That's right! It was a cunning trap though! As soon as I picked up the statue, a trapdoor opened up underneath me, and I fell into a darkened room, full of
textByHash.05631d2cd5fdddb83689fefe9b99f14a6c6ecdb0: bones, and the biggest meanest tribesman you ever did see!
textByHash.7383ba2c4f502f5e4186ec1abdfe35d03421e140: Huge he was, and real ugly looking! He had this huge sword too, and he came running towards me screaming something in some language I didn't understand!
textByHash.8e8353f1eb863bf361c4a264b2fa002d5431cf18: Well, while he was screaming at me, and swinging his sword around in front of me, trying to intimidate me before attacking, I cooly and calmly...
textByHash.1d7c16e6424dfd82f70b5f9c2b14d42b46a88b6a: All the while, knowing that if I died in this spot nobody would even know where I was...
textByHash.e1e1bfee2474f89b43b287248b2ecad721e9deb4: I knew, if this tribesman killed me, that would be the end of Asgarnia Smith, and all of his treasure hunting...
textByHash.609ca1fbe04eb8f4fd9bdee760fe773a96d6969d: Well, while he was screaming and waving his sword around, I stood behind a nearby stalactite, then shot him with my shortbow.
textByHash.2528cbfeb1905bd0a41307280eb2ac595369a611: And that was how I recovered the Golden Monkey of Tee-Ta-Toh.
textByHash.c1e5538180548d51c6ca2d9dcd5f96df2cb306d2: Thinking of joining your pal Asgarnia in the wonderful world of treasure hun- um, archaeology, huh?
textByHash.d980fab2b966965c3e4557133f1be09ac40dc8fe: Well, it all started the day I was fishing in catherby, funnily enough.
textByHash.c5fd8ac5e39288cd85ac24bd9343cf626b82b4a6: Ah yes, I remember it well... I was just sat there, hoping to catch a bass or two, when I made the most amazing discovery!
textByHash.90783d3a087777265f10acec8675f35372c7c3a7: In my fishing nets, I had found an ancient casket, marred and worn by centuries underwater!
textByHash.caa1f83ef3a54a5e302c931d8b4ffbc10be3564b: With trembling hands, I slowly and cautiously opened it, wondering what ancient secret may have been kept inside...
textByHash.c53aac5993e4e77945331ac4bb8f7f31c65973aa: What valuable heirloom may have been dredged up by my nets that had not decayed underwater long ago...
textByHash.fcc39d363589bd2fee0a71f42befb959d343515a: And you know what I found inside?
textByHash.598d79e5ad570dbb00151262a567bc02f5bbb295: I found 320 coins! Just like that! Just by fishing I had found 320 coins! 320 coins! Can you believe it?
textByHash.309f3f31a2d43f0427750e780063521046355d0f: Well, it might not seem much to us now kid, but back then, it was a revelation!
textByHash.f76201d7a8e894ce007cdfa95e7d53b38344e3e3: Why waste my time fishing when there were secret treasures from long ago hidden around the world?
textByHash.6f879bceb395c65574aa77f2591bc5e72a05a304: I immediately applied to the Museum of Varrock as a field agent, and the rest is history!
textByHash.cbe20165eb0c52ff84876b0a4e44e77c132ddc88: Hey, thanks kid. Looking snazzy is an important part of being a treasure hunter!
textByHash.de0db847f150eab98eb9de9c8dfe4853c65236a1: Ah, don't sweat it kid. I'll be honest with ya - I'm already rich many times over, and its more the kicks of being a treasure hunter I do it for than the actual cash!
